theme: Design a delay network for a target decay
title: Feedback Delay Network: Matching a Decay Curve and an Echo-Density Curve
strategies: trivial: reproduce the checker's own baseline (multiples of Lmin, constant gain 0.7)
greedy: evenly-spaced (step=2) delay lengths + a single global gain fit from the endpoint decay level -- the textbook single-exponential recipe

strong: pairwise-coprime greedy delay-length selection (controls echo density via reachable-sum density) combined with a two-gain-group closed-form fit (fast/slow modal decay rates from local target-curve slopes) -- exploits that decay shape and echo density are two independent, separately-controlled descriptors

other viable directions: local hill-climbing refinement of gains/lengths from a coprime seed; more than two gain groups for a smoother multi-stage decay; prime-power delay lengths for maximal pairwise coprimality
